name: overpass_turbo
title: Query Geographic Data with Overpass Turbo
description: Find hospitals, EV charging stations, and schools in any city using plain English queries, then export as GeoJSON.
prompt: Go to overpass-turbo.eu, find all hospitals in Hyderabad, view on map, query EV charging stations, export as GeoJSON

steps.caption: Step 1: Open Overpass Turbo and load the map + query editor workspace
steps.description: Open Overpass Turbo and load the map + query editor workspace
steps.narration: Opening shot. Overpass Turbo gives us a map, an editor, and a city-sized question.

steps.caption: Step 2: Split view: query editor on left, map + controls on right
steps.description: Split view: query editor on left, map + controls on right
steps.narration: This framing helps immediately. Logic on one side, evidence on the other.

steps.caption: Step 3: Write a Hyderabad hospitals query in Overpass QL using a fixed city bounding box
steps.description: Write a Hyderabad hospitals query in Overpass QL using a fixed city bounding box
steps.narration: Our first question is simple. Show us hospitals across Hyderabad, and keep the search area explicit.

steps.caption: Step 4: Run query to load all mapped hospitals as explorable markers
steps.description: Run query to load all mapped hospitals as explorable markers
steps.narration: Now we run it. This is the part where the city answers back.

steps.caption: Step 5: Use 'zoom to data' to jump map viewport directly onto Hyderabad results
steps.description: Use 'zoom to data' to jump map viewport directly onto Hyderabad results
steps.narration: Instead of hunting around the map, we jump straight to where the results live.

steps.caption: Step 6: Click map markers to inspect feature tags like name, address, and amenity type
steps.description: Click map markers to inspect feature tags like name, address, and amenity type
steps.narration: A marker turns the dots into actual places, with names, tags, and context.

steps.caption: Step 7: Open Data tab to inspect raw tabular output and OSM attributes
steps.description: Open Data tab to inspect raw tabular output and OSM attributes
steps.narration: Data view is the reality check. The map looks nice, but the table tells the truth.

steps.caption: Step 8: Switch back to Map view to visually compare coverage across neighborhoods
steps.description: Switch back to Map view to visually compare coverage across neighborhoods
steps.narration: Then we go back to the map, because spatial patterns are easier to feel than to read.

steps.caption: Step 9: Replace with EV charging station query to analyze clean-mobility readiness
steps.description: Replace with EV charging station query to analyze clean-mobility readiness
steps.narration: Same workflow, new question. This time we ask about charging stations instead of hospitals.

steps.caption: Step 10: EV charging points rendered; now compare spread versus healthcare locations
steps.description: EV charging points rendered; now compare spread versus healthcare locations
steps.narration: Now the comparison gets interesting. Different services, same city, one visual frame.

steps.caption: Step 11: Run school query to map education infrastructure in the same city boundary
steps.description: Run school query to map education infrastructure in the same city boundary
steps.narration: Next layer: schools. The story gets better when we ask the city more than one question.

steps.caption: Step 12: Add pharmacy query as a fourth dataset to build a richer city-services comparison
steps.description: Add pharmacy query as a fourth dataset to build a richer city-services comparison
steps.narration: Now pharmacies join in, and the map starts feeling less like a query result and more like a city portrait.

steps.caption: Step 13: Single editor workflow lets you iterate quickly across multiple urban datasets
steps.description: Single editor workflow lets you iterate quickly across multiple urban datasets
steps.narration: This is the quiet win. One editor lets us iterate across datasets without changing tools or losing context.

steps.caption: Step 14: Open Export options for downstream GIS and analytics workflows
steps.description: Open Export options for downstream GIS and analytics workflows
steps.narration: Once the map makes sense, export matters. A good demo should hand off cleanly to the next workflow.

steps.caption: Step 15: Export supports GeoJSON, KML, GPX, and raw OSM for different tools
steps.description: Export supports GeoJSON, KML, GPX, and raw OSM for different tools
steps.narration: GeoJSON is the practical handoff here. Easy to carry into GIS tools, code, or a web map.

steps.caption: Step 16: Tutorial complete: from iterative query editing to exportable, reproducible geo-data
steps.description: Tutorial complete: from iterative query editing to exportable, reproducible geo-data
steps.narration: Final shot. We went from query editing to clean, exportable geo-data.
